description: Converts the active layer to grayscale using weighted luminance (0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B)
Inverts every RGB channel of the active layer (255 − value)
Applies a 3×3 box blur averaging kernel to the active layer
Applies a classic sepia tone transformation using fixed RGB coefficients
Inverts each RGB channel when its value is below 128, creating a solarization effect
Reduces each RGB channel to 4 discrete levels, creating a poster-like banding effect
Applies an emboss relief kernel and shifts by 128 to produce a 3D raised effect
Detects edges using a Laplacian kernel, outputting bright edges on a dark background
Sharpens the active layer using a 3×3 kernel (center 5, cardinal neighbours −1)
Converts the active layer to grayscale using equal-channel average (R+G+B)/3
Stretches each RGB channel independently from its min–max range to full 0–255
Sets the alpha channel to 255 (fully opaque) for every pixel on the active layer
